flashcards.front: What is the formula for permutations of n objects with identical items of k types?
How does combination with repetition differ from classical combination?
flashcards.back: Allows multiple selections of same item. Formula: C(n+r-1, r)

flashcards.front: What does the Stars and Bars theorem calculate?
flashcards.back: Number of ways to distribute r identical items into n distinct bins (non-negative integers)

flashcards.front: When solving distribution problems, what indicates we should use combinations vs permutations?
flashcards.back: Use combinations if order doesn't matter, permutations if order matters in distribution

flashcards.front: What is the application of generalized permutations in cryptography?
flashcards.back: Calculating possible key arrangements with repeated characters in passwords/keys

flashcards.front: How many solutions exist for x₁ + x₂ + x₃ = 10 where xᵢ ≥ 0?
flashcards.back: C(10+3-1, 10) = 66 solutions using Stars and Bars

flashcards.front: What constraint changes the Stars and Bars formula to C(r-1, n-1)?
flashcards.back: When each bin must contain at least 1 item (xᵢ ≥ 1)

flashcards.front: Why are generalized combinations essential in inventory management systems?
flashcards.back: For calculating stock arrangement possibilities with identical products

flashcards.front: What is the multinomial coefficient used for?
flashcards.back: Dividing n objects into groups of specified sizes (generalized permutation)

flashcards.front: How does backtracking utilize generalized permutations?
flashcards.back: Generating all possible arrangements with repetition constraints in algorithm design

flashcards.front: What real-world problem models as a permutation with identical objects?
When would you use inclusion-exclusion with combinations?
flashcards.back: When distributions have minimum/maximum constraints per bin
